examples: He followed the fresh hoofprints to find the deer.
I seem to make fresh mistakes every time I start writing.

definitions.text: (of food) Not dried, frozen, or spoiled.
examples: After taking a beating in the boxing ring, the left side of his face looked like fresh meat.
I brought home from the market a nice bunch of fresh spinach leaves straight from the farm.

definitions.text: (of plant material) Still green and not dried.

examples: We are fresh out of milk.

definitions.text: A rush of water, along a river or onto the land; a flood.
A stream or spring of fresh water.
The mingling of fresh water with salt in rivers or bays, as by means of a flood of fresh water flowing toward or into the sea.
To flood or dilute an area of salt water with flowing fresh water.
Rude, cheeky, or inappropriate; presumptuous; disrespectful; forward.
examples: No one liked his fresh comments.

definitions.text: Sexually aggressive or forward; prone to caress too eagerly; overly flirtatious.
examples: Hey, don't get fresh with me!
